author: Muhammad Usman
title: Tuhfa-tul-Islam
gloss: A booklet describing some of the important Muhammadan doctrines such as prayers, fasts, resurrection, heaven, hell, etc., in the form of questions and answers.
notes: Islamic catechism (Q&A form) in Sindhi via Pokhar Das.

title: Sri Mad Bhagwat
gloss: Translation into Sindhi of the 6th, 7th, 8th and 9th Skandhas of Shrimat Bhagwat, by Chauth Ram.
notes: Bhagavata Purana skandhas in Sindhi — Hindu scripture in the Sindhi market, printed at the (Muslim-named) Islamia Press.

author: Ali Baba, Maulvi
title: Qissa Mandud Shah
gloss: The story of Solomon and Mandud, the king of ants. The latter with his army of insects destroyed the huge army of Solomon because the latter was proud of it. On repentance God forgave Solomon and restored the dead soldiers to life.
publisher: Abdul Aziz, Ghulam Muhammad, Muhi-ud-din, Booksellers
copyright: M. Abdul Aziz, Ghulam Muhi-ud-din, Booksellers, Baramula; No. 21, dated the 14th January, 1910.
notes: Rare KASHMIRI-language entry (Solomon and the ant-king), from Baramula — a language-market edge (cf. the Tibetan/Multani rarities in Q4).

author: Azizullah
title: Rauzat-us-Shuhada
gloss: Verses describing the martyrdom of Imams Hasan and Husain, grandsons of the prophet Muhammad.
notes: Karbala martyrology (Rauzat al-Shuhada) in Kashmiri, from Srinagar. Dual date: 1327 Hijri.

author: Natha Mal
title: Rahnuma-e-Freehand Drawing
gloss: A Guide to Freehand Drawing for School and College Students.
copyright: L. Natha Mal, Drawing Master, Government High School, Sialkot; No. 3, dated the 15th February, 1910.
notes: Section carries '(Designed for educational purposes.)'. Freehand-drawing manual by a Government High School drawing master — the URDU—ART section as school art pedagogy (cf. the music/art manuals in Q4).

author: Ahmad Hussain
title: Halat-i-Sa'di
gloss: A brief account of the life of Shaikh Sa'di, the famous poet and moralist of Persia who lived more than six hundred years ago.
notes: Life of Sa'di, 3rd edition — the Sa'di cult (whose Bostan/Gulistan pervade this year's translations) extends to biography.
